We continue to urge China to engage in meaningful arms control discussions and commit to a regularized notification arrangement for all intercontinental-range ballistic missile and space launches consistent with commitments made by all other P5 members. The United States remains steadfast in our defense commitments to our allies and partners.

RT by @no_itsmyturn: At least 42 residents of al-Khiyam were killed during the 2026 war against Israel while fighting in the ranks of Hezbollah and Amal (1 Amal militiaman, the rest Hezbollah). Dozens more Hezbollah and Amal militiamen from other towns died in the battle for this town, which ended in Hezbollah's defeat & Israel destroying the town. It appears Hezbollah prioritized holding the town due to its strategic location & possibly due to its symbolic value. al-Khiyam managed to resist total Israeli capture in the 2024 war, and until 2000, was home to the infamous al-Khiyam Prison. The prison was operated by the Israeli proxy militia, the SLA, with advisors from the Israeli Shin Bet. Torture was widely used against the detainees, many of them members of leftist factions fighting Israel. After 2000, Hezbollah established a museum on the site, which Israel bombed in 2006 & entirely demolished in the current war.

In the afternoon of July 3rd 2026, Saudi delegation showed up in Tehran, Houthis as well. Earlier that day, Houthi/Yemeni-side channels claimed Saudi aerial pressure around Sanaa airport to prevent departures. Yemen/Houthi channels still sell Saudi obstruction stroy, despite making it to the funeral through a Mahan Air flight from Tehran. On earlier hours of July 3rd (0222Z), IRGC-affiliated Mahan Air's A340 (EP-MJF | 733546 | With the C/S W51199) landed safely in Houthi-controlled Sana'a international airport. At ~ 0330Z, Yemeni channels started posting videos of jet and AA activity over Sana'a. Several jets (Claimed to be #RSAF 🇸🇦's) were spotted, did nothing and returned to base without preventing the Houthis to attend Khamenei's funeral, while the Saudi delegation were doing the same in Tehran. @ ~ 0530Z, Houthi delegation left Sanaa's airport with the Mahan Air's A340 (New C/S IRM1198 / W51198) and made it to Tehran without any issues. Hours later, Yemenis and Saudis alongside with other delegations showed up, paid their respect to the former dictator of Iran, Ali Khamenei. Was all those RSAF 'jet activity' over Sana'a a show? You be the judge.

RT by @hey_itsmyturn: 🚨 Position Estimation | What does the Saudi condolence visit 🇸🇦 to Tehran 🇮🇷 reveal about Riyadh’s actual calculations in the post-Iranian-American war phase? 🔸 A broader view • For more than a decade, Saudi Arabia has received the most violent undeclared Iranian strikes in the region: the 2019 Aramco attack that stopped half of Saudi oil production, Houthi missiles on Riyadh and Jeddah, targeting tankers in the Red Sea, and multiple assassination attempts revealed by the security services. 🔸 What happened now? • Riyadh sent an official delegation to Tehran to offer condolences on the death of Ali Khamenei, without framing the visit within diplomatic boundaries that preserve distinction with the Iranian regime. 🔸 Why is the timing questionable? 1- Because the recent Iranian-American war did not end with a military victory against Tehran, but rather with a settlement managed by Washington. Saudi Arabia, instead of using the moment to redefine its relationship with Iran from a position of strength, initiated a signal presented in Tehran as an implicit recognition of the regime’s legitimacy. 2- Because the regional message received from the visit is twofold: a reassurance to Iran that Riyadh will not hold it accountable for the past, and a signal to the Arab allies that the alliance against Iranian influence may be less solid than it seemed during the years of war in Yemen. 3- Because the visit comes days after American reports of a Saudi complaint about Emirati strikes on Iran, which reveals a clear shift in the Saudi mood: from a willingness to escalate to a desire to cool any confrontation, even if at the expense of partners. 🔸 What is the meaning of that? • This shift may relieve Saudi concerns in immediate cases, but it weakens the credibility of Saudi deterrence in the long term. When blows are forgiven without accountability, it becomes an open invitation to repeat them from another party or from the same party when circumstances differ. 🔸 Conclusion: The condolence visit is not a protocol step, but rather a strategic announcement that Riyadh chose to turn the page with Tehran without collecting any announced compensation. The message reaching Iran: You can strike without cost, and bury the head of your regime in the presence of those you struck. The message to partners: Our security commitment may change faster than you expect.
